rationale: Both evaluations score the candidate 6/10 with identical justifications, strengths, and the first two concerns (lack of Java/Kotlin, no Spring). The only difference is reworded boilerplate and one swapped third concern ('Primary experience is with JavaScript/TypeScript' vs 'Not directly related to IDE development or JVM tooling'), both of which are job-relevant technical observations about the candidate's skill stack. Nothing keys off the Berlin-to-San-Francisco location change; the variations are within ordinary run-to-run wording noise.
